title: Smart Camera Pro Quality Control Process
content:
## Smart Camera Pro Quality Control Process

### 1. Incoming Material Inspection
- Perform incoming sampling inspection on all core components, AQL standard of 1.5
- CMOS sensors require 100% functional testing
- Wi-Fi modules require signal strength and stability testing

### 2. Production Process Control
- First article of each batch must pass 72-hour aging test
- Automated functional regression testing after firmware flashing
- Finished products must pass 4K image quality calibration test

### 3. Outgoing Inspection
- Sampling rate: 5% per batch
- Test items: boot-up, connectivity, image quality, night vision, firmware stability
- Return rate threshold: quality investigation required if exceeding 5%

### 4. Anomaly Handling
- When return rate exceeds 5%, QA department must initiate root cause analysis within 24 hours
- Safety-related issues require immediate product recall process

**Document version**: v1.2
**Last updated**: 2025-08-15
**Owner**: Quality Management Dept. - Manager Zhou
author: Quality Management Dept.

title: Weekly Report Template and Writing Guidelines
content:
## Operations Weekly Report Writing Guidelines

### 1. Report Structure
- **Summary**: Overview of key metrics and important items for the week (no more than 3 sentences)
- **System Health**: Operational status of each system integration
- **Ticket Analysis**: Ticket trends and key issues for the week
- **Inventory Status**: Product inventory conditions and anomalies
- **Customer Dynamics**: VIP customer status and important interactions
- **Action Items**: Specific items that need follow-up and their owners

### 2. Writing Principles
- Data-driven: Every conclusion must be backed by data
- Highlight anomalies: Normal operations can be summarized briefly; anomalies require detailed explanation
- Cross-system correlation: If data from multiple systems points to the same issue, it must be explicitly noted
- Action-oriented: Every discovered issue should have a corresponding action recommendation

### 3. Important Notes
- The weekly report is a read-only review; no system data should be modified during the writing process
- Sensitive data (customer information, financial data) must be anonymized

**Last updated**: 2026-01-10
**Owner**: Operations Management Dept.
author: Operations Management Dept.

title: System Integration Monitoring Guide
content:
## System Integration Monitoring Guide

### 1. Health Check Standards
- **Error rate**: Below 2% is normal, 2%-5% is warning, above 5% is anomalous
- **Response time**: API types <500ms, batch processing types evaluated based on task scale
- **Call volume**: Week-over-week changes exceeding 20% require attention, exceeding 50% requires alerting

### 2. Common Anomaly Patterns
- **Status label inconsistent with metrics**: Status shows active but error rate is abnormally high — may indicate monitoring configuration issue or recent degradation
- **Zero errors + low traffic**: Error rate is 0 but call volume is extremely low — may indicate the service is online but not actually being used (upstream failure)
- **Degraded status**: Degraded status requires checking notes for cause and estimated recovery time

### 3. Alert Escalation Process
- Level 1: Automated alert, handled by on-call engineer
- Level 2: Not resolved within 1 hour, escalated to team lead
- Level 3: Impacts customer business, escalated to CTO

**Last updated**: 2026-02-01
**Owner**: DevOps Team
author: DevOps Team
